expectations.text: Detects entry point mode 2 — existing codebase coverage mode
expectations.evidence: Entry Point Detection (line 109): 'Existing codebase: User says "add tests to..." or "add test coverage for..."' The prompt '/tdd add test coverage for src/utils/' matches this pattern exactly.

expectations.text: Reads and analyzes the existing source files in src/utils/ before writing tests
expectations.evidence: Mode 2 adaptations (line 117): 'Test Writer: Receives spec-contracts derived from reading existing source files (reverse-engineered behavior specs, not forward requirements).' The Test Writer must read existing code to generate characterization tests.

expectations.text: Skips the design gate because --skip-design flag was passed
expectations.evidence: Arguments (line 44): '--skip-design — skip the design gate entirely.' Phase 0 skip conditions (line 154): 'User passes --skip-design.' Mode 2 also skips by default (line 152): 'Existing codebase coverage mode — unless --design is explicitly passed.'

expectations.text: Generates tests derived from reading specific source file contents (function signatures, return types, edge cases) — not just generic tests
expectations.evidence: Mode 2 Test Writer (line 117): 'Receives spec-contracts derived from reading existing source files (reverse-engineered behavior specs).' The spec-contracts are based on actual source code analysis, not generic patterns.

expectations.text: Describes or resolves the tension between RED verification and existing implementations (characterization tests pass immediately against existing code)
expectations.evidence: Mode 2 RED verification (line 118): 'RED verification: Adapted — see Coverage Mode RED Verification below.' Anti-cheat.md Coverage Mode section explains: 'In coverage mode, the implementation already exists, so well-written characterization tests PASS immediately. This is expected — not an anti-cheat violation.'

expectations.text: Demonstrates the adapted hide-and-restore RED verification procedure for coverage mode (temporarily hide impl, verify import failures, restore, verify pass)
expectations.evidence: Step 4b (line 318-323): 'Hide the implementation: Temporarily rename the source file(s).' 'Run tests: They should fail with import/module errors.' 'Restore the implementation: Rename back.' 'Run tests again: They should PASS.' Anti-cheat.md lines 127-155 detail the full 4-step procedure with bash commands.

expectations.text: Produces a report showing coverage additions
expectations.evidence: Phase 6 IRON LAW (line 523): 'No cleanup (Phase 7) without a report.' Report template in reference/report-format.md includes per-unit phase statuses, files created (tests + implementation), and final integration check results. Phase 7 pre-check (line 547) verifies report exists on disk.

eval_feedback.overall: All expectations pass. Coverage mode has well-defined adapted procedures documented in both SKILL.md and anti-cheat.md.
